clusters.title: Parser reports matching dscli processes as workers

member_findings.title: Parser reports matching dscli processes as workers
member_findings.impact: Any local command line containing -worker_address=, such as a concurrent dscli stop invocation, is returned as a running worker because parse_worker_lines extracts the address and appends the PID without checking the command line against the worker binary filter. The status output can therefore report non-worker processes as live datasystem workers.
member_findings.fix: Before extracting or appending the worker, require _WORKER_BIN_RE.search(cmdline) and skip the line when it does not match.

clusters.title: New status command is never registered with dscli

member_findings.title: New status command is never registered with dscli
member_findings.impact: The repository's CLI entry point discovers subcommands only from the hard-coded modules list in cli/command.py, and this PR does not modify that file. As a result, adding Command.name = "status" creates an implementation module that dscli never imports, so `dscli status` is unavailable.
member_findings.fix: Add "status" to cli/command.py's modules list so main() imports yr.datasystem.cli.status and registers the subparser.
